message:
feat(string): add strnstr (BSD bounded substring search) (bd-voxzi)

BSD strnstr(haystack, needle, n) — like strstr but searches at
most n bytes of haystack, returning a pointer to the first
occurrence of the (still-NUL-terminated) needle, or NULL if not
found. Used by many BSD ports; without it programs that depend
on it will not link against frankenlibc.

* core::string::str::strnstr returns Option<usize>; bounds the
  haystack scan at min(n, strlen(haystack)) and treats an empty
  needle as a Some(0) match.
* string_abi::strnstr shim handles NULL pointers (NULL haystack →
  NULL; NULL needle → haystack, matching existing strstr) and
  bounds the haystack scan via a new core_strnlen helper that
  never reads past `n` for unterminated buffers.

12 core unit tests + 10 abi integration tests. The pointer-arithmetic
test verifies the shim never reads past `n` for an unterminated
buffer (BSD spec requirement). Clippy-clean, fmt-clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
